summary: Added a CLI integration test that verifies the concurrent-process lock behavior end-to-end. The test pre-creates a .cron-lock.lock file owned by the live test-runner PID, then spawns `agt cron-tick` as a subprocess and asserts it exits 0 with 'already running' — confirming the second concurrent process bails out immediately without running a feature. The implementation (lockFile with timeout:0 at cron.mjs:76-82) was already in place; this task closes the gap with a real filesystem lock test.
